arts.ttl: Training Report: Bayern Munich’s João Palhinha joins team training; Daniel Peretz back in three weeks; Hiroki Itō works on the ball; and MORE!
arts.sum: Hiroki Itō training with the ball earlier today ⚽ [ @BILD ] pic.twitter.com/1PupdFJX5W Daniel Peretz says he'll be back in 3 weeks pic.twitter.com/FIwRZ9O5nm

arts.ttl: Daily Schmankerl: Erling Haaland and Manchester City ruin Project 2026 for Bayern Munich; Leroy Sané to Premier League?; Borussia Dortmund selling Gio Reyna, Karim Adeyemi, but buying Marcus Rashford?; and MORE!
arts.sum: Well, pour one out for #Project2026 as the centerpiece of that plan — Manchester City star Erling Haaland — has inked a nine-year extension with the club, which likely makes him unattainable for Bayern Munich : Bayern Munich star Leroy Sané will be a free agent this summer and if the Bavarians are reluctant to ink him to a new deal, the winger will reportedly have suitors from the Premier League:

arts.ttl: “Patience is not one of my qualities” — Bayern Munich exec Max Eberl gives contract talk updates
arts.sum: “But during the contract talks, whether with Alphonso [Davies], Jamal [Musiala], Josh [Kimmich] or Leroy [Sané], we try to act with facts. Bayern is trying to extend a number of players, including Alphonso Davies, Jamal Musiala and Joshua Kimmich.

arts.ttl: Bayern Munich boss Vincent Kompany ready to bump heads with formidable adversary in Wolfsburg’s Ralph Hasenhüttl
arts.sum: Bayern Munich did not take too long to get going after the break as it tallied five goals against Hoffenheim on Wednesday, something the coach thinks is a product of the players’ overall quality. Bayern Munich coach Vincent Kompany is ready to face VfL Wolfsburg, but also knows that he will have a formidable adversary in the opposing coaching box.

arts.ttl: Bayern Munich fans deliver rousing tribute to Franz Beckenbauer during Hoffenheim win
arts.sum: It was a snowy night at the Allianz Arena, but the Bayern fans were warmed up considerably by the stellar display shown by the team, who racked up five early goals to sail to a 5-0 victory. For those who don’t know, Gute Freunde kann niemand trennen is a song sung and recorded by Beckenbauer himself in 1966, when he was in his early twenties.

arts.ttl: Update: After tension and rigmarole, Alphonso Davies and Bayern Munich nearing four-year agreement
arts.sum: Davies’ agent Nedal Huoseh and Bayern director of sport Max Eberl discussed the new contract today and could finalize an agreement as soon as next week. Davies’ agent Nedal Huoseh and Bayern director of sport Max Eberl discussed the new contract today and could finalize… pic.twitter.com/yIe3BYZ0uQ

arts.ttl: Alphonso Davies to renew Bayern Munich contract - First domino to fall?
arts.sum: Alphonso Davies is set to commit his future to Bayern Munich. Transfermarkt can confirm an initial report by The Athletic that the Canadian men’s national team star is set to sign a new...

arts.ttl: Report: Jamal Musiala will extend his contract at Bayern Munich and get elusive release clause
arts.sum: Negotiations between Bayern and Musiala have advanced, but the deal is not done yet, as there is no agreement on the salary yet. According to a report from Sky Sport journalists Florian Plettenberg and Kerry Hau, Jamal Musiala will get the hefty contract he desires — and also the elusive release clause, too.

arts.ttl: Report: Christopher Nkunku’s move to Bayern Munich depends on Mathys Tel, may wait for summer
arts.sum: Christopher Nkunku joining Bayern in January is currently unlikely due to Chelsea’s €70m asking price and the fact Mathys Tel wants to stay at Bayern, even though Nkunku’s agent Pini Zahavi wants to make the deal happen. Should a January move prove to be impossible, Nkunku remains an option for Bayern in the summer [@Plettigoal]

arts.ttl: Bayern Munich fend off Real Madrid to secure Alphonso Davies’ future
arts.sum: According to David Ornstein, Alphonso Davies is close to agreeing on a contract extension with Bayern Munich. For over twelve months, there has been great uncertainty regarding the future of…
